title: Podman still displays shows deleted pod / container
body:
### Issue Description

Podman throws an error when I attempt to delete a pod + containers created with ``podman play kube file.yml``. It appears to be similar to #2900, but which was patched three major versions ago; that seemed to have been caused by corruption of the boltdb. Is there a guide to fixing a borked boltdb (for example with [boltbrowser](https://github.com/br0xen/boltbrowser)), or is a ``podman system reset`` advised? I would prefer not having to do the latter.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Generate a pod (in my case with ``podman play kube ...``)
2. Delete the pod (``podman pod rm ...``)
3. Pod still exists.

### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman pod exists privatebin ; echo $?
0


$ podman pod rm --force privatebin
Error: error freeing lock for container 31fc3611c36edb487aebfa0b1af35039aaa0b1dd66389f04f186df96f3f77024: no such file or directory


$ podman pod list
POD ID        NAME        STATUS      CREATED      INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
f7a64c8cb3cf  privatebin  Error       2 hours ago  31fc3611c36e  1


$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED      STATUS      PORTS                                        NAMES
31fc3611c36e  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-1673617532              2 hours ago  Removing    0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8443->443/tcp  f7a64c8cb3cf-infra


$ podman container exists f7a64c8cb3cf-infra ; echo $?
0
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected a ``podman pod rm -f $pod`` to remove the pod and all of its containers.
